subject.description: Late-30s man, slightly disheveled, dark circles under eyes. Rumpled button-down shirt, sleeves rolled to elbows. Three days of stubble. Sitting hunched forward at a cluttered desk.
subject.action: Staring across three open screens simultaneously, right hand hovering over a keyboard, left hand holding a cold coffee cup mid-air. Head turned slightly left, eyes scanning tabs. Jaw barely dropped — the half-second of holding three things in mind at once and losing them.

setting.location: Open-plan office desk covered in printed reports, sticky notes, two open laptops. Third monitor shows a dashboard with red numbers. Two coffee cups visible — one empty, one half-full. Scattered papers, a forgotten pen uncapped.
setting.time_of_day: Late afternoon — harsh overhead fluorescent light, no natural light visible.

composition.camera_angle: Eye-level, slight high angle — camera just above eye-line
composition.framing: Small medium shot. Crown with headspace at top. Cut below nipples. Subject slightly left of center, rule of thirds. Never cut in flesh.

lighting.type: Harsh overhead fluorescent office light, no diffusion, slight shadows under eyes and chin.
lighting.color_grading: Cinematic teal-orange grade — blue-pushed shadows, warm protected skin tones. Slightly desaturated except skin.

mood: Coordination exhaustion — the specific tiredness of someone who has been switching context all day and has nothing to show for it.
aesthetic_style: Shot on Sony A7 with 80mm lens. Cinematic teal-orange color grade — blue-pushed shadows, warm protected skin tones. Real environment. Skin pores visible. Individual stubble hairs visible. Subject caught mid-thought, NOT posed. Looks like a still from a prestige documentary or drama series.
